title: Mossel Bay swimmers tackle the ‘Kilimanjaro of South African rivers’
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Six determined Mossel Bay open-water swimmers are set to take on the legendary 14km Kromme River Challenge in St Francis on 27 September.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - Six determined Mossel Bay open-water swimmers are set to take on the legendary 14km Kromme River Challenge in St Francis on 27 September."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Training in icy winter waters as low as 11°C and completing long build-up swims as with a 10km Goukou River session, the team, Jurie Zietsman, Nelmarie Broekhuizen, Lelané Muller, René van den Berg, Adri van der Merwe and Colleen Olivier, have shown remarkable resilience and teamwork.","html":"Training in icy winter waters as low as 11°C and completing long build-up swims as with a 10km Goukou River session, the team, Jurie Zietsman, Nelmarie Broekhuizen, Lelané Muller, René van den Berg, Adri van der Merwe and Colleen Olivier, have shown remarkable resilience and teamwork."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"As they face unpredictable currents, tides and winds, they carry the pride of Mossel Bay, inspiring the local community with their dedication."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Credit is also due to enthusiastic local swimming coach Bronwyn MacDonald, who skillfully managed the group’s weekly training programme.","html":"Credit is also due to enthusiastic local swimming coach Bronwyn MacDonald, who skillfully managed the group’s weekly training programme."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Mossel Bay swimmers tackle the ‘Kilimanjaro of South African rivers’
